mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in greater than 70% of observations during recovery phases, or short-term return autocorrelation completely dominates the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 55% of high-inflation months, or the market prices in rate hikes without corresponding equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based economic strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the correlation between the two series breaks down causing persistent signal noise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening residential construction activity and broader economic confidence, which consistently correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts data shows no predictive power for equity returns, with a walk-forward Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal is consistently overwhelmed by short-term market noise.
